questions.question: Who is a consumer?

options.B: Person who buys goods or services

questions.explanation: A consumer is a person who buys goods or services for personal use.

questions.question: The Consumer Protection Act was first passed in India in:
questions.explanation: The Consumer Protection Act was passed in 1986.

questions.question: Right to Safety protects consumers against:
questions.explanation: Consumers have the right to be protected from hazardous goods.

questions.question: Which mark ensures product quality in India?
questions.explanation: ISI mark certifies product quality.

questions.question: Right to Information means consumers can:
questions.explanation: Consumers have the right to know product details.

questions.question: Which organization works for consumer awareness?
questions.explanation: Consumer forums help resolve disputes.

questions.question: Agmark is related to:
questions.explanation: Agmark certifies agricultural products.

questions.question: Which is a consumer right?
questions.explanation: Consumers have the right to choose among products.

questions.question: MRP stands for:
questions.explanation: MRP means Maximum Retail Price.

questions.question: Right to Seek Redressal means:

options.A: Right to complain and get compensation

questions.explanation: Consumers can file complaints for compensation.

questions.question: Which body deals with consumer complaints at district level?
questions.explanation: District Forum handles local complaints.

questions.question: Which right protects against unfair trade practices?
questions.explanation: Right to be informed protects against unfair practices.

questions.question: Which logo indicates food safety certification?
questions.explanation: FSSAI ensures food safety standards.

questions.question: Consumer courts are set up to:
questions.explanation: Consumer courts resolve disputes between buyers and sellers.

questions.question: Right to Choose means:
questions.explanation: Consumers can choose from available products.

questions.question: Hallmark is used for:
questions.explanation: Hallmark certifies purity of gold.

questions.question: Which of the following is unfair trade practice?
questions.explanation: False advertisement misleads consumers.

questions.question: Right to Consumer Education means:
questions.explanation: Consumers should be aware of their rights.

questions.question: Which document is proof of purchase?
questions.explanation: Bill is proof for complaint.

questions.question: Right to Safety mainly applies to:
questions.explanation: Consumers should be protected from harmful goods.

questions.question: Which of the following is a quality mark?
questions.explanation: ISI mark shows standard quality.

questions.question: Consumers should check expiry date for:
questions.explanation: Food items have expiry dates for safety.

questions.question: Which level of court handles highest consumer disputes?
questions.explanation: National Consumer Commission is highest level.

questions.question: The main aim of Consumer Protection Act is:
questions.explanation: The Act protects consumer rights.

questions.question: Which right ensures freedom from monopoly?
questions.explanation: Right to choose protects against monopoly practices.
